summary: High‑energy Turn On Your Love Light showcase recorded from a solid soundboard source, with a few early sloppiness but overall an exciting 1970 performance.
review: The April 11 1970 Fillmore West show is remembered chiefly for a blistering Turn On Your Love Light that lets Pigpen (and “Bobby”) loose, complete with perfect Shine‑on‑Me harmonies and wild organ jams. The soundboard recording is praised as clear and well‑balanced for its era, though one reviewer notes the song’s intro feels a bit muddy and lacking fire. Overall the performance shines despite a rough start.

ai_rating.rationale: Five detailed reviews give an average of 4.4 stars; most highlight outstanding playing and a good‑quality soundboard mix, with only one mildly negative comment about the early part of the song.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced for a 1970 tape; reviewers cite good era‑appropriate sound, occasional feedback and spacing, but no major dropouts or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set builds into a powerful Turn On Your Love Light that dominates the evening, with Pigpen’s organ and vocal antics providing the high point; early moments are described as sloppy, but the energy picks up dramatically toward the climax.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Bob Weir) is noted for “going nuts” and delivering a fierce finish on Turn On Your Love Light.
